opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — our baby has been screaming non-stop since lunchtime and nothing calms him. His toes are looking purple and puffy, and the plaster cast on his leg has started smelling like rotten meat.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The crying has gone on for about 6 hours and it's getting worse, and in the last hour the cast has started to smell really bad — we can't wait until his clinic appointment.

symptom_narrative.onset: the crying started around lunchtime, about 6 hours ago; the toes started looking dusky and swollen about 3 hours ago and the smell from the cast came on about an hour ago
symptom_narrative.character: high-pitched, inconsolable screaming — not his normal grizzle at all
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we've ever seen him, he won't settle for anything
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: he's in casts on both legs; it's the right leg toes that look purple and the right cast that smells
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — cuddling, feeding and rocking aren't working
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps going on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: his toes on the right foot look dusky and swollen and feel cool, and there's a foul smell from the top of the right cast; he feels a bit sweaty
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's been in the casts for 3 weeks for his feet and has been fine until today
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can't be settled, hasn't napped, and we're frightened something is badly wrong

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: born with both feet turned inward and downward (clubfoot) — he's having his feet slowly straightened with plaster casts that get changed each week
past_medical_history.how_managed: weekly long-leg plaster casts (he's in week 3), under the children's orthopaedic clinic

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: we gave a dose for the crying but it didn't help

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 5-week-old baby, first child, lives at home with both parents; the house is warm and he's in loose clothing over the casts

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: no concerns other than the clubfoot being treated

objective_data_offered.value: the toes poking out of the right cast look dusky/purple and puffy compared with the left
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent's naked-eye impression under home lighting; not a clinician assessment

objective_data_offered.value: the right toes feel cooler to touch than the left toes when we hold them
there is some swelling of the leg just above the top edge of the right cast
a strong foul/rotten smell coming from the top opening of the right cast
37.8 degrees on our home ear thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an unvalidated home infant thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: he is sweaty, red-faced and crying vigorously and cannot be soothed
